intro: Malaxis sulamadahensis is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Malaxis sulamadahensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, Malaxis sulamadahensis is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used in various herbal remedies, such as teas and tinctures, to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of infections.
